title: Gonioanthela hilariana
taxonomy: Gonioanthela hilariana is classified within the family Gonioanthelidae, which is a group of myxomycetes, a type of organisms that are a combination of animal and fungus. These organisms are often referred to as slime molds due to their ability to move and feed like animals, but also have characteristics similar to fungi. Gonioanthela hilariana, in particular, is a genus of myxomycetes that are found in various parts of the world, including Australia and Asia. The exact classification of Gonioanthela hilariana may vary depending on the source and the current understanding of the organism's characteristics. Further research is necessary to determine the most accurate classification of this species.
